Short-answer item with papers provided. Both parts are closed-book solvable from the stem plus general reasoning and are directly anchored by the evidence: (1) Evidence states mixture explosion limits cannot be approximated from individual-component limits (including via weighted methods), so using ethylene alone as a conservative proxy is not admissible; evidence also notes ethylene has highest single-component propensity but that does not transfer to mixtures. (2) Evidence explicitly states LFP vent-gas explosion tendency peaks at 50–100% SOC and is higher there than at overcharged states, so reducing/setting SOC to 110% to move outside the peak region is invalid and reverses the stated SOC–tendency relationship. No internal ambiguity or contradiction with the evidence bundle.
